feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entry.content: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

entry.title: 3 Eagles projected to land among Madden 27's highest-rated rookies
entry.content: The Eagles' first three selections from the 2026 NFL Draft are all expected to rank among Madden 27's 100 top-rated first-year players.

entry.title: France bid adieu to Didier Deschamps and a legacy tied to one question
entry.content: Les Bleus exit at the semi-finals, ending a run of winners, finalists and the final four, but, as Richard Jolly writes, Deschamps might be both the most decorated Frenchman on the global stage and an underachiever

entry.title: Braves ace Chris Sale sends fiery message to No. 1 prospect Cam Caminiti regarding his future
entry.content: Chris Sale has been the ace for the Atlanta Braves since he arrived. He just sent a message to the top prospect that should fire him up during his minor league days.

entry.title: Declan Rice offers England fitness hope ahead of the Argentina match
entry.content: Declan Rice’s injury has dominated much of England’s build-up to their World Cup semifinal against Argentina, and the midfielder’s fitness remains one of the biggest talking points ahead of toda...

entry.title: How to watch England v Argentina, team news, predicted line-ups and more
entry.content: England and Argentina will renew one of international football’s fiercest rivalries on Wednesday night with a place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on the line.Thomas Tuchel’s side booked their p...

entry.title: “I’d hoped that entry into the Champions League would have been enough” — Gasperini admits sales needed as Man United target Kone
entry.content: AS Roma manager Gian Piero Gasperini has suggested that Manu Kone will leave the club amid reports that Manchester United have agreed a deal for the midfielder.Manchester United are rumoured to have r...

entry.title: India vs England 2nd ODI 2026 lineups as Men in Blue look to seal the series in Cardiff
entry.content: With India already one-up in the three-match series, here's a look at the expected lineups for the second ODI as the Men in Blue look to seal the series.

entry.title: Predictions, Odds, Analysis! UFC Oklahoma City, X-Factor
entry.content: This weekend (Sat., July 18, 2026), Ultimate Fighting Championship (UFC) will venture forth to the Paycom Center in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma for UFC OKC. Unfortunately, one of the best fights of the card imploded just las week when Kevin Holland withdrew from his scheduled clash with top rising prospect Jacobe Smith. Fortunately, the high-profile main […]

entry.title: Spotify is expanding free access to kid-friendly managed accounts
entry.content: Spotify is expanding free access to managed accounts for under-13s.

entry.title: XGIMI Elfin Flip 4K review: An ultra-portable laser projector with sharp video quality
entry.content: XGIMI's Eflin Flip 4K is a portable, stylish projector that provides outstanding image quality at a great price.

entry.title: US producer prices unexpectedly fall in June
entry.content: US producer prices unexpectedly fall in June

entry.title: What Makes Birkenstock Holding plc (BIRK) an Attractive Bet?
entry.content: What Makes Birkenstock Holding plc (BIRK) an Attractive Bet?

entry.title: PPI wholesale inflation falls 0.3% in June thanks to lower gas prices
entry.content: PPI wholesale inflation falls 0.3% in June thanks to lower gas prices

entry.title: Samsung's new foldable display technology is harder to damage and resists creases
entry.content: Samsung says its next foldables will have less visible creases thanks to its new titanium display tech.

entry.title: Daily Spotlight: State of Global Demand for U.S. Debt
entry.content: Daily Spotlight: State of Global Demand for U.S. Debt

entry.title: Withings' new ultra-premium scale goes on sale today
entry.content: Withings' new $600 bathroom scale promising clinical-grade monitoring is finally available to buy.

entry.title: Google will allow third-party app stores on Android next week
entry.content: Third-party app stores are coming to Android on July 22.

entry.title: Blanche to testify today at Senate confirmation hearing for attorney general
entry.content: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is set to appear before the Senate Judiciary Committee at 9 a.m. for his confirmation hearing to lead the Justice Department in a permanent capacity.

entry.title: Jay Clayton, Trump's DNI nominee, to testify at Senate confirmation hearing
entry.content: Clayton, currently the U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York, is expected to succeed Bill Pulte, Trump's controversial acting director of national intelligence.

entry.title: 15 international streaming series to keep your World Cup fever alive
entry.content: For six weeks, soccer made the U.S. the center of worldwide celebration. Keep the post-World Cup blues at bay by watching these 15 international series.

entry.title: Inside the states' case to block the Paramount-Warner Bros. merger
entry.content: Winning a restraining order will be a crucial test for California Atty. Gen. Rob Bonta and his group. Some observers see the states' efforts as an uphill climb because the U.S. Justice Department last month approved the merger.

entry.title: Millions of dollars were wagered on 'Love Island.' Why Kalshi wants more trades in reality TV
entry.content: 'Love Island' marked Kalshi's largest TV market to date, with over $77 million in trading. With more success in its culture markets, the overall percentage of female traders on the prediction market platform has doubled in the last year.

entry.title: 'Mexodus,' a live-looping, hip-hop history lesson, reenergizes the American musical
entry.content: Brian Quijada and Nygel D. Robinson bring their award-winning musical 'Mexodus,' a live-looping, hip-hop musical about a fugitive slave who finds freedom south of the border, to Pasadena Playhouse.

entry.title: Southampton sign Dobbin from Villa for £9m
entry.content: Southampton complete the signing of forward Lewis Dobbin from Aston Villa for a fee of almost £9m.

entry.title: Sunderland sign Belgium World Cup defender Meunier
entry.content: Experienced Belgian defender Thomas Meunier signs for Sunderland after his contract with Lille comes to an end.

entry.title: Stark County Amateur Golf Championships registration set to close
entry.content: The final day to register to play in this year's Stark County Amateur Golf Championships is Wednesday, July 15.

entry.title: Estupiñán to Aston Villa and beyond: Milan prepare exits to raise €150m
entry.content: Aston Villa target Pervis Estupiñán isn’t the only player expected to leave Milan this summer, as the Rossoneri are preparing for more sales, hoping to raise €150m this summer, reports Gazzetta....

entry.title: Borussia Dortmund make Ibrahim Mbaye enquiry
entry.content: Borussia Dortmund have made an enquiry about Paris Saint-Germain forward Ibrahim Mbaye (18), according to a report from Fabrizio Romano.Mbaye has accrued plenty of international experience in recent ...

entry.title: Report: Real Madrid priorities emerge after major contract announcement
entry.content: Real Madrid transfer latest as Jude Bellingham form and Aurelien Tchouameni renewal define Mourinho’s thinkingReal Madrid approach every summer under a spotlight bright enough to illuminate every co...

entry.title: World Cup 2026: England braced for Argentina tie - will Three Lions reach the final?
entry.content: England prepare to face Argentina in World Cup semi-final for chance to face Spain in Sunday's final.

entry.title: Little League Baseball New Jersey Section 3 Tournament schedule, scores
entry.content: Little League Section 3 U12s schedules, scores: Toms River, Freehold Township, North Howell, Nottingham and Clark.

entry.title: CDC director nominee Erica Schwartz faces Senate for confirmation hearing
entry.content: Dr. Erica Schwartz, Trump's nominee for CDC director, faces the Senate health committee for her confirmation hearing on July 15. If confirmed, she will lead an agency dealing with workforce and leadership shortages, and new layers of political review.

entry.title: More than 1,600 cases of intestinal illness cyclosporiasis reported in the U.S.
entry.content: The CDC has confirmed more than 1600 cases of the parastic infection from cyclospora and is working to confirm at least 5,00 thousand more, and 144 ppl have been hospitalized. Case counts are "unusually high" and expected to continue to increase in the coming weeks.

entry.title: Wheelchair users say lack of competition in repair market leads to long wait times
entry.content: People who use wheelchairs say simple repairs can take months to complete and replacement parts can be very expensive. In part, they blame two private equity firms that control most of the market.

entry.title: Ebola is spreading faster in eastern Congo than it can be tracked, as deaths pass 700
entry.content: Eighty percent of new Ebola cases in eastern Congo are emerging from unknown chains of transmission, according to WHO, a sign the outbreak is spreading faster than health officials can track.

entry.title: China’s Economy Grows at Weakest Pace Since 2022
entry.content: China’s gross domestic product expanded 4.3% in the second quarter, as a dismal domestic economy offset surging exports.

entry.title: By Blessing Corruption, Todd Blanche Has Disqualified Himself From the Job He Wants
entry.content: By Blessing Corruption, Todd Blanche Has Disqualified Himself From the Job He Wants

entry.title: China Aggressively Patrols Disputed Waters. Now the U.S. Coast Guard Is Moving In.
entry.content: Several ships have been pulled from the Middle East to help challenge Beijing’s assertion of power in the Pacific.

entry.title: This California Town Voted Its Council Out of Office. They’re Refusing to Leave.
entry.content: Avenal is in political crisis as four recalled officials, including the mayor, cling to control in one of the more unusual power struggles to hit the state.

entry.title: Seattle residents say they are in 'survival mode,' selling homes as high prices squeeze middle class
entry.content: Seattle residents say they are in 'survival mode,' selling homes as high prices squeeze middle class

entry.title: The U.S. and Iran Reckon With Shrinking Options to End the War
entry.content: Concerns about the midterms and rising oil prices constrain President Trump while Tehran is gambling that it can outlast a naval blockade.

entry.title: Pressure Builds Over Israel’s Treatment of Palestinian Prisoners
entry.content: Reports from Israeli groups and interviews with former detainees detail alleged violence, medical neglect and starvation.

entry.title: Texas drenched by heavy rains as forecasters warn more storms could bring floods
entry.content: Heavy downpours in South Texas washed out highways and stranded motorists Tuesday as forecasters warned that a threat of more severe weather could bring dangerous flooding to already drenched counties near the border with Mexico

entry.title: 1 missing and 17 rescued after a boat fire near Alcatraz Island off San Francisco
entry.content: Authorities say a person is missing and 17 others have been rescued after a fire on a pontoon boat off the coast of San Francisco

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
True love is not something that comes everyday, follow your heart, it knows the right answer.
Lucky Numbers: 13, 54, 44, 32, 8, 44

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Before you throw away a sticky note, run it between the keys on your keyboard to collect crumbs and fluff.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: Almonds are a member of the peach family.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Celestine Chua
entry.content: “There's no next time. It's now or never.” — Celestine Chua

entry.title: U.A.E. Rewarded With Coveted AI Chips for Supporting U.S. War in Iran
entry.content: The Gulf state’s flagship AI firm can freely buy chips and has a plan to become a U.S. company.

entry.title: ICE temporarily halts vehicle pursuits after two fatal shootings in Maine and Texas
entry.content: ICE temporarily halts vehicle pursuits after two fatal shootings in Maine and Texas

entry.title: Writers Guild of America seeks to block Paramount's buyout of Warner in latest merger challenge
entry.content: The Writers Guild of America became the latest group to challenge Paramount's $81 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery on Tuesday, filing a lawsuit that seeks to block the merger on the grounds it would cause "specific harm" to movie and TV writers working across the U.S.

entry.title: Pat Oliphant, fearless Pulitzer-winning political cartoonist, dies at 90
entry.content: Political cartoonist was known for caricatures of U.S. and world leaders.

entry.title: March for democracy planned for anniversary of historic MLK-led 1963 Washington march
entry.content: A large coalition led by the Rev. Al Sharpton and the family of the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. will mark the anniversary of the historic March on Washington next month with a march for democracy

entry.title: 74-year-old Florida man becomes oldest inmate executed in the state’s modern history
entry.content: 74-year-old Florida man becomes oldest inmate executed in the state’s modern history

entry.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
entry.content: OpenAI's first hardware device is reported to be a screenless, AI-guided smart speaker that can move. Weird enough for you?

entry.title: Yahoo College Fantasy Football: Texas A&M's top five players
entry.content: Here are the five highest-ranked Texas A&M players in Yahoo Sports College Fantasy Football 2026 preseason projections.

entry.title: Spain deliver World Cup masterclass against France to reach final
entry.content: Spain ended France's dream of a third World Cup triumph on Tuesday, taming their star-studded attack in a 2-0 victory to reach the final against England or Argentina.The encounter takes place 40 years after Diego Maradona's infamous "Hand of God" goal helped Argentina to a 2-1 win at the 1986 World Cup in Mexico.

entry.title: Chelsea in advanced talks to sign Manchester United’s Melvine Malard
entry.content: Chelsea are in advanced talks over a transfer for Manchester United forward Melvine Malard. Talks are taking place between the clubs over a deal which would be worth in the region of £850,000 plus add-ons, with personal terms not expected to be an issue. It would represent a club-record sale for United and the highest fee a women’s club has received for a player with a year left on their contract. The 26-year-old is entering the final year of her contract and it had become increasingly clear tha

entry.title: Ukraine Sea Drone Sinks Russian Ship Near Compound Said to Be Linked to Putin
entry.content: The attack showed the extent to which the drones have helped Kyiv exercise greater control in the Black Sea.

entry.title: Gibraltar ushers in a new era as British territory's border fence with Spain is removed
entry.content: Thousands of people who travel every day between the southern tip of Spain and the British territory of Gibraltar will no longer have to cross a physical border beginning Wednesday.

entry.title: 30 Most Important Bears of 2026: Devin Bush (No. 15)
entry.content: We've reached the halfway point of our Most Important Bears series with Devin Bush, who is tasked with transforming the linebacker position in 2026.

entry.title: Jacob Misiorowski's All-Star Game red-carpet moment includes his dog
entry.content: Jacob Misiorowski won't get to flex on the All-Star Game this year, but that didn't stop him from throwing heat on its red carpet.

entry.title: Rams announce when they will reveal 2 new alternate uniforms
entry.content: The Rams will reveal two new alternate uniforms on July 23, which will give them 5 sets for 2026.

entry.title: Scott Bryce, actor known for 'As the World Turns,' dies at 68
entry.content: Actor Scott Bryce, known as the original Craig Montgomery on soap opera 'As the World Turns,' has died. He was 68.

entry.title: Geno Smith will not be charged in domestic dispute
entry.content: Geno Smith may be in the clear from domestic abuse charges.

entry.title: New diagnoses slow in NYC Legionnaires' disease outbreak; source still unclear
entry.content: A Legionnaires' disease outbreak in a New York City neighborhood now counts 60 cases, but new diagnoses are slowing, health officials said Tuesday. They reported progress on inspections for the disease-causing bacteria but still haven't pinpointed a source.

entry.title: Wizards rookie AJ Dybantsa shut down for rest of NBA Summer League
entry.content: No. 1 overall pick AJ Dybantsa has been shut down by the Wizards for the remainder of the NBA Summer League in Las Vegas.

entry.title: ICE Suspends Traffic Stops After Two Shooting Deaths
entry.content: Two men were killed roughly a week apart in Texas and Maine during immigration-enforcement operations.

entry.title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
entry.content: The measure also allows states to opt out if they take action before the federal law is enacted.

entry.title: U.S. Reimposes Iranian Blockade as Trump Scraps Hormuz Toll
entry.content: Plus, Kevin Warsh says the Fed has ’no tolerance’ for high inflation, and the most expensive dinosaur sells for $50.1 million.

entry.title: Spain reaches World Cup final with dominant 2-0 win over France
entry.content: In a match Tuesday featuring two of soccer's biggest heavyweights, Spain put in a masterful performance, frustrating France to the tune of a 2-0 win to advance to Sunday's World Cup final​.

entry.title: Russian Soyuz rocket launches fresh crew to space station
entry.content: The new crew will replace three other Soyuz fliers wrapping up a 240-day stay aboard the International Space Station.

entry.title: "Miracle on the Hudson" pilot, Capt. "Sully," shares Alzheimer's diagnosis
entry.content: Capt. Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger, who famously landed a passenger jet on the Hudson River in 2009, said he has been diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease.

entry.title: Iraq seizes mounds of gold and millions in cash in corruption crackdown
entry.content: An anti-corruption probe in Iraq leads to seizure of 825 pounds of gold and tens of millions in cash from lawmakers and government officials.

entry.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
entry.content: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

entry.title: New York City Officials Inspecting Other Buildings After Midtown High Rise Buckled
entry.content: The buildings department is looking at projects of those involved in the Manhattan incident last week.

entry.title: Key Moments: Supreme Court Justices Barrett and Kagan Testify Before Congress
entry.content: The high court sent a delegation to present a budget request and answer lawmakers’ questions for the first time since 2019.

entry.title: WGA sues Paramount, claiming Warner Bros. acquisition would take away jobs
entry.content: The Writers Guild of America sued Paramount on Tuesday, alleging its planned acquisition of Warner Bros. violates federal antitrust law. The lawsuit comes a day after California Atty. Gen. Rob Bonta and 11 other state attorneys general sued Paramount.

entry.title: U.S. resumes naval blockade against Iran, launches new airstrikes
entry.content: The United States on Tuesday resumed its naval blockade against Iran and launched new strikes against the country. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports and retired Col. Mark Cancian joins with analysis.

entry.title: Houston prosecutor says ICE tactics "in no way resemble" traditional policing
entry.content: Asked about the recent ICE-related fatal shootings, Harris District Attorney Sean Teare says the actions of federal immigration agents "in no way resemble" the tactics of "every law enforcement agency" he's worked for.

entry.title: Space-based aspect of Trump's 'Golden Dome' shield gets boost with $1.75 billion in new contracts
entry.content: The Trump administration's Golden Dome missile defense shield project is expanding its secretive network of space-based, missile-detecting satellites with a $1.75 billion dual contract for two defense technology firms, despite concerns on Capitol Hill about ballooning costs.

entry.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
entry.content: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

entry.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
entry.content: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.

entry.title: Cyclospora outbreaks causing diarrhea in US expected to continue through August
entry.content: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention says it is scouring food supply chain for culprit, which may be lettuceWhat is cyclosporiasis, the parasitic illness causing ‘explosive’ diarrhea?Federal health officials have said they expect the outbreaks of cyclospora – a parasite that causes watery, explosive diarrhea – across the US to continue through August as they scour the food supply chain for the culprit.The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has identified 1,645 lab-confirmed cases of the parasite; reported that 145 people have been hospitalized; and reported that they have a backlog of 5,100 cases that require more analysis, including interviews. Continue reading...

entry.title: Lucid Motors denies report it’s considering bankruptcy
entry.content: The company said the "rumors are completely false" after its stock sank more than 50% on a report that it was weighing the option.

entry.title: Warren Buffett excludes Gates Foundation from his annual donations of Berkshire stock
entry.content: Warren Buffett omitted the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts.

entry.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
entry.content: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

entry.title: Derision at US chain Chipotle’s plan to sell its Mexican food in Mexico
entry.content: One social media user described the opening of its first outlet in Monterrey as ‘like the dog teaching a duck to fly’A US chain’s plan to sell its version of Mexican food to Mexicans with a first branch south of the border has prompted bemusement, skepticism and anger among local people.Chipotle Mexican Grill, known for its customisable burritos, tacos and bowls, has more than 4,000 locations worldwide and announced on Tuesday that it was expanding into Mexico in what it described as a significant milestone. Continue reading...

entry.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
entry.content: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

entry.title: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
entry.content: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

entry.title: Watch: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh testifies to House Financial Services committee
entry.content: In remarks prepared for the appearance, Warsh promised a vigilant fight to return inflation to the Fed's 2% target.

entry.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: IBM, Goldman Sachs, CleanSpark, HCA Healthcare & more
entry.content: These are some of the stocks posting the largest moves midday.

entry.title: DeepSeek reportedly in talks to raise $1.5B, then IPO
entry.content: DeepSeek, the Chinese large language model developer, is said to be preparing for a 2027 IPO debut as it also looks to raise around $1.5 billion in new funds at a $71 billion valuation.

entry.title: Protect public health policies from lobbying firms | Letter
entry.content: Caroline Cerny, Prof Sir Ian Gilmore and Katharine Jenner welcome the ethics watchdog’s recommendations to improve transparency around lobbyingFrom tobacco and alcohol to unhealthy food, there is extensive evidence that companies whose profits depend on the sale of harmful products have repeatedly sought to influence the policies designed to protect public health.Research from around the world has documented efforts to deny, dilute and delay measures aimed at reducing harm, while promoting approaches that are more favourable to commercial interests. Too often, that lobbying takes place behind closed doors with no opportunity for scrutiny. Continue reading...

entry.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
entry.content: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI tools.

entry.title: The Battle for Hormuz: Trump Shifts Into Dangerous New Phase in Iran War
entry.content: President Trump’s third major shift in military strategy comes as he searches for a way to turn the tables on Iran in the nearly five-month-old war.

entry.title: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
entry.content: Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history.

entry.title: Trump Backs Off 20% Strait of Hormuz Fee
entry.content: The decision comes just a day after the president floated the idea of charging other countries shipping cargo through waterway that Iran has sought to control

entry.title: Tuberculosis outbreak at Colorado ICE jail sickens at least 12 detainees
entry.content: The reported infections are the latest in a series of health concerns at the federal immigration jail in AuroraAt least 12 people detained at a federal immigration jail in Colorado have contracted tuberculosis in recent days, according to testimony from inside the facility where dozens of others have reportedly been placed in quarantine.Those affected by the outbreak are also being made to endure their isolation without air conditioning, one detainee who has been at the facility in Aurora since December told the Guardian, through his partner, in a telephone call on Monday afternoon. Continue reading...
